data.label: Internet
data.description: Understand the foundational concepts of how the internet works, including what HTTP is, how browsers function, and the roles of domain names and hosting.
objectives: Explain the client-server model.
Describe the purpose of HTTP/HTTPS.
Understand what a domain name and hosting are.
Explain the basic function of a web browser.

data.label: HTML
data.description: HTML (HyperText Markup Language) is the backbone of all web pages. It provides the structure and semantic meaning of your content.
objectives: Learn the syntax of basic HTML tags.
Understand the importance of semantic HTML for accessibility and SEO.
Create forms and understand different input types.
Know the fundamentals of SEO basics.

data.label: CSS
data.description: CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) is used to style and layout web pages — for example, to alter the font, color, size, and spacing of your content, split it into multiple columns, or add animations and other decorative features.
objectives: Learn basic CSS selectors and properties.
Understand the box model.
Create layouts using Flexbox and CSS Grid.
Implement responsive design using media queries.

resources.title: CSS-Tricks - A Complete Guide to Flexbox
CSS-Tricks - A Complete Guide to Grid

data.label: JavaScript
data.description: JavaScript is the programming language of the web. It enables you to create dynamically updating content, control multimedia, animate images, and much more.
objectives: Understand variables, data types, and operators.
Learn control flow (if/else, loops).
Work with functions, objects, and arrays.
Understand asynchronous JavaScript (Promises, async/await).
Learn how to manipulate the DOM.
Make network requests using the Fetch API.

data.label: Version Control Systems
data.description: Version control systems are essential tools for tracking and managing changes to your codebase, especially when collaborating with others. Git is the most popular VCS.
objectives: Understand the purpose of version control.
Learn basic Git commands (clone, add, commit, push, pull).
Know how to use a VCS hosting platform like GitHub.

data.label: Pick a Framework
data.description: Frameworks provide structure and pre-written code to build your applications more efficiently. React, Vue, and Angular are the most popular choices.
objectives: Understand the concept of component-based architecture.
Learn state management within the chosen framework.
Build a multi-page application with routing.
Fetch and display data from an API.

data.label: Writing CSS
data.description: Modern frontend development often uses CSS frameworks or methodologies to write scalable and maintainable styles. Tailwind CSS is a utility-first framework that has gained immense popularity.
objectives: Choose a CSS framework or methodology.
Learn how to use utility classes (Tailwind) or preprocessors (Sass).
Understand how to customize and extend the chosen styling approach.

data.label: Testing
data.description: Testing your code is crucial for building robust and reliable applications. This includes unit tests for individual functions, integration tests, and end-to-end tests that simulate user behavior.
objectives: Understand the difference between unit, integration, and E2E tests.
Write basic tests using a framework like Jest or Vitest.
Learn how to use a testing library like React Testing Library or Cypress.

data.label: TypeScript
data.description: TypeScript is a superset of JavaScript that adds static types. It helps you catch errors early, write more reliable code, and improve developer experience on large projects.
objectives: Understand basic types (string, number, boolean).
Learn how to type objects and arrays.
Use interfaces and types to define data structures.
Configure a project to use TypeScript.

data.label: Server-Side Rendering (SSR)
data.description: Server-Side Rendering frameworks (also called meta-frameworks) build on top of UI frameworks like React to provide features like routing, server-rendering, and optimizations out of the box, improving performance and SEO.
objectives: Understand the benefits of SSR.
Learn a meta-framework like Next.js (for React) or Nuxt.js (for Vue).
Create a full-stack application with API routes and server-rendered pages.
